segments.text: My son, pay attention to my wisdom, turn your ear to my words of insight,
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: that you may maintain discretion and your lips may preserve knowledge.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For the lips of the adulterous woman drip honey, and her speech is smoother than oil;
but in the end she is bitter as gall, sharp as a double-edged sword.
Her feet go down to death; her steps lead straight to the grave.
She gives no thought to the way of life; her paths wander aimlessly, but she does not know it.
Now then, my sons, listen to me; do not turn aside from what I say.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Keep to a path far from her, do not go near the door of her house,
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: lest you lose your honor to others and your dignity to one who is cruel,
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: lest strangers feast on your wealth and your toil enrich the house of another.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: At the end of your life you will groan, when your flesh and body are spent.
How I hated discipline! How my heart spurned correction!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I would not obey my teachers or turn my ear to my instructors.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And I was soon in serious trouble in the assembly of God's people.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Drink water from your own cistern, running water from your own well.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Should your springs overflow in the streets, your streams of water in the public squares?
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let them be yours alone, never to be shared with strangers.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: May your fountain be blessed, and may you rejoice in the wife of your youth.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: A loving doe, a graceful deer— may her breasts satisfy you always, may you ever be intoxicated with her love.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Why, my son, be intoxicated with another man's wife? Why embrace the bosom of a wayward woman?
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For your ways are in full view of the Lord, and he examines all your paths.
The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare them; the cords of their sins hold them fast.
For lack of discipline they will die, led astray by their own great folly.
